message:
perf(btree): replace O(n*k) brute-force segment training with O(n) slope-constraint PLA

The `train_piecewise_linear` function builds a Piecewise Linear
Approximation (PLA) for the learned index, mapping key values to
positions. The old algorithm used a brute-force approach: for each
candidate segment endpoint, it computed the linear fit and then checked
ALL points in [seg_start, seg_end] for max error, making it O(n*k)
where k is the average segment length.

Replace this with the classic slope-constraint (aka "optimal PLA")
algorithm from the streaming/time-series literature. The key insight is
that maintaining running min/max slope bounds is sufficient to determine
when a segment must end:

  For each new point i, compute the slope range [p_min, p_max] that
  would keep point i within max_error of the line from seg_start.
  Intersect this with the running [slope_min, slope_max]. If the
  intersection is empty, the segment must end at i-1.

This reduces training to a single O(n) pass since each point is visited
exactly once. The final segment slope is clamped to the feasible range,
which was not guaranteed by the old algorithm (it just used the
endpoint-to-endpoint slope without checking feasibility).

Also handles the degenerate case of duplicate keys (dx=0) explicitly:
if more than max_error consecutive keys have the same value, force a
segment break since no linear function can map them all within error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
